zh.Cogan, Glamorgan, Wales: 威爾士格拉莫爾根可甘
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard de Cogan於1164年出生在威爾士格拉莫爾根可甘時，他的父親Milo (Miles) de Cogan29歲，母親Christiana Paynell5歲。他至少有1個兒子。他去世於1238年在英國戴文享年74歲。

zh-hans.Cogan, Glamorgan, Wales: 威尔士格拉莫爾根可甘

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Cogan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of Jewish (from Ukraine) Kogan, from the Russian pronunciation of Cohen .

Irish and Welsh (of Norman origin): habitational name from Cogan near Cardiff, which may have been named with a Welsh word meaning ‘bowl, depression’. In 1166 Milo de Cogan, held two fees near Penarth (Glamorganshire) of William earl of Gloucester. Members of this Norman family probably account for the name's appearance in 13th-century Berwickshire. By this time the family was also present in Ireland, associated with counties Limerick and Cork. It may have been the Irish branch that later brought the name to southwest Scotland.
Irish (Leitrim): variant of   Coogan  .

variants.name: Cogen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Dorith.  German Erwin.
Variant of   Cogan  .

variants.name: Coggin
meanings: English: variant of   Cogan  .

variants.name: Cohen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Meyer, Isadore, Emanuel, Moshe, Shlomo, Myer, Avi, Yehuda, Chaim.
Jewish: from Hebrew kohen ‘priest’. Priests are traditionally regarded as members of a hereditary caste descended from Aaron, brother of Moses. See also Kaplan .
Irish (Galway and Sligo): variant of   Coyne  .

variants.name: Coggins
meanings: English: variant of Cogan , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Coggan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Brigid, Conal, Seamus.  Jewish Hyman.
English: variant of   Cogan  .
Americanized form of Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic) Kogan, from the Russian pronunciation of Cohen .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Cogan was born in 1164, in Cogan, Glamorgan, Wales, his father, Milo (Miles) de Cogan, was 29 and his mother, Christiana Paynell, was 5. He had at least 1 son. He died in 1238, in Devon, England, at the age of 74.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard de Cogan est né en 1164, à Cogan, Glamorgan, Galles, son père, Milo (Miles) de Cogan, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Christiana Paynell, avait 5 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1238, à Devon, Angleterre, à l’âge de 74 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard de Cogan 1164 in Cogan, Glamorgan, Wales geboren wurde, war sein Vater Milo (Miles) de Cogan 29 und seine Mutter Christiana Paynell 5. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1238 im Alter von 74 Jahren in Devon, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Cogan è nato il nel 1164 nel Cogan, Glamorgan, Galles, il suo padre, Milo (Miles) de Cogan, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Christiana Paynell, aveva 5 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1238, nel Devon, England, all'età di 74 anni.

ja.Bampton, Devon, England: イギリスデボンバンプトン
ja.Cogan, Glamorgan, Wales: ウェールズグラモーガンコガン
ja.lifeSummary: Richard de Coganが1164年にウェールズグラモーガンコガンで生まれた時，父Milo (Miles) de Coganは29歳，母Christiana Paynellは5歳であった。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1238年にイギリスデボンで74歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Cogan(이)가 1164년 웨일스 글램르건 코건에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Milo (Miles) de Cogan(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Christiana Paynell(은)는 5세였다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1238년 영국 데폰에서 74세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Cogan nasceu em 1164, em Cogan, Glamorgan, Gales, seu pai, Milo (Miles) de Cogan, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Christiana Paynell, tinha 5 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1238, em Devon, Inglaterra, com 74 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard de Cogan родился в 1164 г. в местности коган, глэморган, Уэльс, его отцу по имени Milo (Miles) de Cogan было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Christiana Paynell, 5. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1238 г. в местности девон, Англия в возрасте 74 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard de Cogan nació en 1164, en Cogan, Glamorgan, Gales, su papá, Milo (Miles) de Cogan, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Christiana Paynell, tenía 5 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1238, en Devon, Inglaterra, a la edad de 74 años.
